assumptions: TargetVault.agentManager is the gate contract address (set at vault construction, immutable thereafter)
Solidity 0.8.20 integer overflow checks are sound
block.timestamp drift is bounded by Ethereum consensus rules (≤15s)
The agentKey may be arbitrarily adversarial (jailbroken, compromised, hallucinating); the gate's guarantees are over the agent's BEHAVIOR space, not its INTENT

walkaway.explanation: RuntimeGate has no admin role, no proxy pattern, no upgrade path. maxOutflowPercent, whitelistedSpenders, agentKey, and vault are all immutable post-construction. The bounded (not full) classification reflects that the GATED protocol stack (TargetVault) has an owner — the user — who can withdraw funds at will. This is correct: the user retains exit. The agent's authority is bounded by the gate's predicates; the user's authority is unbounded by design (they own their vault). The walkaway is bounded by the fact that the agent's authority cannot be removed without redeploying — there is no key-rotation function. In practice this is acceptable because the gate's predicates already foreclose the most dangerous agent actions.
dependencies_disclosed: TargetVault must be deployed with agentManager set to this gate's address; if set to anything else, the gate's predicates are not enforced

The gate enforces predicates on the agent's calls to itself; it cannot prevent calls made directly to the vault by an entity that has somehow acquired vault.agentManager privileges (which by construction is only this gate)

evidence.justification: Lean 4 theorem gate_bounds_single_transfer in demos/agent_gate/proof/Containment.lean establishes that any applied transfer is bounded by maxOutflowPercent * vaultBalance / 100. The theorem holds for ANY agent behavior — adversarial or honest. D5 (runtime enforced) because the gate's check is not me

Lean 4 theorems gate_enforces_whitelist and gate_bounds_approval establish that any applied approve is to a whitelisted spender AND bounded by maxApproval. The whitelist is immutable post-construction. D5 because enforcement is at transaction submission time. Zero sorry.

The gate's dailyOutflow + dailyOutflowResetTime tracking provides temporal distinctness for the daily-budget invariant. The Lean theorem gate_single_step_containment establishes per-step correctness; the daily-budget extension is verified via the simulate.py state machine.
